retrieval_text: Belladonna is a medicinal herb profile in the Herbalisti historical corpus. It is represented across 263 works and 1077 source-linked chunks. Recorded variants include Belladonna. Common plant-part co-mentions include Leaf, Root, Resin, Bark, Seed, Flower. Common preparation co-mentions include Extract, Tincture, Plaster, Oil, Powder, Ointment. Common condition co-mentions include Cold or catarrh, Rheumatism, Inflammation, Wounds or ulcers, Fever, Cough. Caution co-mentions include Dose caution, Narcotic or sedative caution, Child-use caution, Allergy or sensitivity caution, Toxicity watch, Fatal-risk warning. Representative source works include Cooley's cyclopaedia of practical receipts and collateral information in the arts, manufactures, professions, and trades, including medicine, pharmacy, hygiene, and domestic economy : designed as a comprehensive supplement to the pharmacopoeia and general book of reference for the manufacturer, tradesman, amateur, and heads of families.; The dispensatory of the United States of America / by Geo. B. Wood and Franklin Bache.; The pharmacopoeia universalis or, complete encyclopedia of the materia medica; contained in the pharmacopoeias of London, Edinburgh, and Dublin as well as of all those of Europe and America ; and of the dispensatories, formularies and chemical works ... With an appendix ... / by A. J. L. Jourdan : Edited by J. Rennie.; The dispensatory and pharmacopoeia of North America and Great Britain / by John Buchanan and John J. Siggins.. This is a retrieval summary of historical co-mentions only, not a medical claim.
